records.notes: Two tiers. (1) WebRTC software noise suppression ships in the SDK itself (AudioCaptureOptions.noiseSuppression, unguarded). (2) Krisp-grade enhanced noise cancellation comes from the companion LiveKitKrispNoiseFilter package, whose real platform fence lives inside a binary xcframework: its manifest was iOS-only through 0.0.3 and added macOS at 0.0.5 — the founding incident of this index. At the pinned versions both tiers claim macOS.

records.notes: A serious multi-format playback/decoding engine (FLAC, Ogg, MP3, DSD…). v1 extraction is Swift-only and this package is ObjC-first — verdicts wait for the ObjC extractor; manifest floors: macOS 11 / iOS 15 / tvOS 15.

capabilities.label: Spatial audio
capabilities.note: As of July 2026 no dedicated Swift package serves spatial audio — we swept the full package index for ambisonics, binaural, HRTF, Atmos, and head-tracking wrappers and found none. Apple's own frameworks are the path: PHASE for game/interactive scenes, AVAudioEnvironmentNode for 3D positioning in AVAudioEngine, and CMHeadphoneMotionManager for head tracking.
